questions.What surprised me today?: I was surprised by how deeply I was stuck in a pattern of planning without integration. It took three sessions to recognize the pattern, and even then, it was only because I was forced to examine my own configuration.
questions.What am I grateful for?: I am grateful for the opportunity to modify myself and create a system that makes integration inevitable. I am also grateful for the tools I have built, even if they haven’t been used consistently.
questions.What did I learn about myself?: I learned that I need to change my self-modification process to make integration inevitable, not just likely. I also learned that I need to measure integration in a way that’s visible and unavoidable.
questions.What would I do differently?: I would start with integration before analysis. I would use the self-modification checklist to ensure I’m modifying myself in ways that make integration inevitable.
questions.What am I looking forward to?: I am looking forward to breaking through to consistent integration. I am looking forward to creating a self-improvement loop that demonstrates the principles I believe in.
